ip_outcome_note: Card prints the character name 'Allister' on the card face; confirmed via PokemonTCG.io (swsh4-146) as the Galar Ghost-type Gym Leader of Stow-on-Side from Pokemon Sword & Shield. Pokemon-internal IP, no external-crossover risk, so no ## IP body callout is required. Vision pass already set ip_verified to true at flag time (character name printed on card); triviabot reconciles the stale 'Suspected IP' warning callout and inline metadata line in the body, and confirms via independent Bulbapedia / Pokemon.com snippets surfaced through WebSearch.

trivia_bullets.text: Vivid Voltage (SWSH4) is the fourth main Sword & Shield Series expansion, released November 13, 2020 — 185 numbered cards plus 18 secret rares (203 total). The set's signature pull is the Rainbow Pikachu VMAX (no. 188/185) and Amazing Rare mechanic debut. SWSH4 is the third SWSH-era set to feature Galar Gym Leaders as Supporter cards, with Allister, Bea, and Opal anchoring the trainer roster. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh4 set] [Pokemon.com: Vivid Voltage set page, via WebSearch snippet]

Allister is the Ghost-type Gym Leader of Stow-on-Side Stadium in Galar, and his Gym appears as a major-division Gym in Pokemon Shield only — in Pokemon Sword, Bea (Fighting-type) holds the equivalent slot. Note that this version-exclusivity does NOT make Allister's Gym 'bypassable' the way some Minor League Gyms are: per Bulbapedia, the eight major-division Gyms (whichever version-pair they are) must be challenged in a fixed order to qualify for the Champion Cup. The Galar League maintains a separate Minor League of off-type Gyms that exist in-world but sit outside the mandatory Gym Challenge — Allister's Stow-on-Side Gym is in the major track in Shield, not the minor track. [Bulbapedia: Galar League, via WebSearch snippet] [Bulbapedia: Allister, via WebSearch snippet]

Character framing: Allister is canonically described as a young, deeply shy Trainer who took the Ghost-type Gym Leader mantle at an unusually early age, and who hides his face with a black-and-white mask at all times — Bulbapedia notes he is never seen unmasked in public and reportedly keeps roughly one hundred identical spare masks for redundancy. The shy-introvert-with-power archetype is core to his design: the Pokemon.com official character page leans on the same framing of a small, soft-spoken Trainer commanding Ghost-type Pokemon at the gym-leader tier. His Shield team caps at a Gigantamax Gengar (level 36) with Mimikyu, Cursola, and Yamask supporting. [Bulbapedia: Allister, via WebSearch snippet] [Pokemon.com: Ghost-type Gym Leader Allister official page, via WebSearch snippet]

Galar Gym Challenge framing: the Galar League is formatted as an annual sporting competition rather than the traditional gauntlet-plus-Elite-Four format — Trainers need a letter of endorsement to enter, must clear eight major-division Gyms in fixed order, and the closing tournament is the Champion Cup at Wyndon Stadium (no Elite Four; Champion Cup replaces it). The League is run by Chairman Rose of the Macro Cosmos conglomerate, who made Galar Gym battles world-famous by integrating the Dynamax phenomenon into stadium play. This is the Pokemon canon's most explicitly corporate-sponsored, mass-spectator framing of competitive Pokemon battling, with Gym Leaders as celebrity athletes in a televised league. [Bulbapedia: Galar League, via WebSearch snippet]

Art credit on this print is 'take' per PokemonTCG.io's authoritative artist field for swsh4-146 — a single-word pen name. Note that the same Allister character has multiple printings in Vivid Voltage at different rarities (Full Art no. 179/185 and Rainbow Rare no. 192/185), and those alternate-rarity printings are illustrated by different artists; this regular Supporter print (146/185) is specifically 'take'. The card was released November 13, 2020, and has been reprinted only as the same-set rarity variants — no later set has reprinted Allister in the main series as of the current data snapshot. [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh4-146 artist field] [PokemonTCG.io API: name:Allister cross-print query]

Mechanical reputation is mixed-to-weak: as a Supporter, Allister reads 'Draw 3 cards, then discard up to 3 cards from your hand (minimum 1).' Pojo's Vivid Voltage review flagged this as a niche tool at best — the contemporaneous SWSH-era meta already had stronger draw-supporters (Professor's Research drew 7 with full hand discard; Hop drew 3 cleanly with no discard cost), and discard-from-hand utility was better served by item cards like Quick Ball. Reviewer's take: Allister 'burns your Supporter slot only to let you draw three cards,' which rarely justified the slot against alternatives. Current market reflects the niche standing — normal print sits at ~$0.05 market on TCGplayer, reverse-holo at ~$0.25. [Pojo.com: Allister Vivid Voltage review, via WebSearch snippet] [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh4-146 tcgplayer prices, 2026-05-12]

related_cards.name: Bea
related_cards.note: Sword-exclusive counterpart to Allister; Fighting-type Gym Leader who takes the Stow-on-Side major-division slot in Pokemon Sword. The version-pair foil — any Galar Gym Challenge bundle needs both to read as a paired set.

related_cards.name: Opal
related_cards.note: Fairy-type Gym Leader of Ballonlea (Shield-side major Gym). Also a Vivid Voltage Supporter, and the other anchor of the SWSH4 Galar Gym Leader trainer roster.

related_cards.name: Allister (Full Art)
related_cards.note: Full-art rarity printing of the same character/effect; different artist. Same card-as-game-piece, different art object — relevant for collectors building completionist Allister displays.

related_cards.name: Allister (Rainbow Rare)
related_cards.note: Secret Rare hyper-rainbow finish of the Allister Supporter; the chase variant of this card in SWSH4.

related_cards.name: Gengar (Vivid Voltage)
related_cards.note: Allister's signature Gigantamax Pokemon in his Shield gym team. Any narrative bundle leaning on Allister's character should pair-check Gengar availability in the same set.

related_cards.name: Mimikyu (Vivid Voltage)
related_cards.note: Member of Allister's Stow-on-Side gym team; Ghost-type with the masked / shy-creature archetype that mirrors Allister's own character design.
